description: Introduction to managerial accounting, focusing on concepts, relationships, and procedures involved in the strategic decision-making process of managers in for-profit business organizations. Covers cost behavior, cost-volume-profit analysis, budgeting, standard costs, variance analysis, and the use of accounting data in planning and control.

professor.name: Heather Altman
professor.topReview: I feel like accounting can't be taught properly in lectures, definitely should be average class sizes. You can tell she does try when teaching. Exams have answer choices that appear when you do the wrong or not enough of the equations which messed me up as this was not the case in accounting 200. I would study for hours and it did not help on exams
professor.aiSummary: Professor Heather Altman teaches in the Accounting Department and maintains a balanced grading approach with moderate difficulty. While students appreciate her efforts during lectures, Altman relies heavily on challenging multiple-choice exams that require precise calculations. Successful students must focus on mastering complex equation structures, as the workload and study requirements are significant.

description: Introduction to financial accounting, including the conceptual framework underlying financial reporting, recording and reporting of business transactions, and preparation and analysis of financial statements (balance sheet, income statement, statement of cash flows). Emphasizes how external users interpret accounting information.

professor.name: Sherri Silverberg
professor.topReview: Is she certified in Accounting? Yes. Does that mean she should be teaching it? No. She has proven she knows the tasks of an accountant but she is not much of a teacher/ educator. She jumps right into material as if it wasn't an introductory course. She works through examples quickly without explaining or teaching us. "Limited Term Adjunct Lecturer"
professor.aiSummary: Sherri Silverberg is an Accounting lecturer known for a fast-paced teaching style that often assumes prior knowledge of the material. Students report a high workload and strict grading, with grades heavily determined by rigorous examinations. Silverberg works through complex examples quickly, making this a challenging option for those new to introductory accounting concepts.

description: Intermediate financial accounting with an in-depth study of the conceptual framework, financial statement preparation, revenue recognition, and accounting for current and long-term assets. Develops the ability to apply Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) to complex transactions.

professor.name: Max Hewitt
professor.topReview: Max has entertaining lectures and gives great feedback following your quizzes and exams. Appreciated all the office hours he held and how quick he got grades back to you. He is a very caring professor that is always happy to help you if needed. The exams are challenging and a lot more difficult that the practice exams but he gives partial credit.
professor.aiSummary: Professor Max Hewitt is a highly-rated accounting instructor known for his engaging lectures and commitment to student support through frequent office hours. While Hewitt provides detailed feedback, students should expect a demanding workload and strict grading standards. Success in his courses requires rigorous preparation for the exams, which are significantly more challenging than the provided practice materials.

description: Advanced financial accounting topics including consolidations, business combinations, foreign currency transactions, partnerships, and accounting for governmental and not-for-profit entities. Builds on intermediate accounting principles to address complex reporting situations.

professor.name: Katie Maxwell
professor.topReview: I did not do very well in this class, although there were many review sessions that I did not attend. Professor Maxwell was very accomodating with extra test time. Show up to class and every review session possible and you can NOT cram for this class. Overall, the material itself was very confusing but, Professor Maxwell was very kind and leniant.
professor.aiSummary: Professor Katie Maxwell is a highly-rated accounting instructor who manages challenging course material with a kind and accommodating approach. While the workload is medium, students should attend review sessions and avoid cramming to succeed on the exam-based assessments. Maxwell provides balanced grading and remains supportive of students navigating the high difficulty of the subject.

description: Principles of federal taxation, with an emphasis on how individuals are taxed. Integrates tax compliance, tax planning, financial accounting, economics, and finance. Students apply the tax framework to a variety of taxation scenarios involving income, deductions, credits, and property transactions.

professor.name: Jeremy Douthit
professor.aiSummary: Professor Jeremy Douthit teaches in the Education department, where he maintains a 3.8/5 rating. His courses feature a project-based assessment style with a medium workload and typical academic difficulty. Students can expect a balanced grading approach from Douthit that aligns with the requirements of his hands-on assignments.

description: Examines the role of the external auditor and the audit process. Topics include professional standards, audit risk, internal controls, evidence gathering, sampling, and audit reports. Develops critical thinking around assurance services and the public accounting profession.

professor.name: Ann Mcgrath
professor.topReview:
Make sure to review those slides before all exams as well as the cases. She gives. VERY vague instructions on the projects, so do your very best to plan ahead with your group. Also, you should aim to participate each class (she keeps track with cards). Participation was worth nearly 15% of total points.

Just make sure you understand the material.
professor.aiSummary: Professor Ann McGrath teaches accounting courses that feature a high workload and medium difficulty level. McGrath focuses heavily on exam-based assessments and class participation, which she tracks daily to calculate a significant portion of the final grade. While information on projects can be vague, students benefit from her lenient grading style and structured lecture slides.

description: First course in business statistics, with an emphasis on techniques for data analysis and inference in management. Covers descriptive statistics, probability, sampling distributions, estimation, hypothesis testing, and regression. Technical material is supplemented with applications drawn from real-world business situations.

professor.name: Brent Marinan
professor.aiSummary: Professor Brent Marinan is a highly-rated Marketing instructor known for maintaining rigorous academic standards. While students appreciate his expertise, the workload and course difficulty remain high due to an exam-based assessment structure and strict grading policies. Marinan is best suited for dedicated students who excel in traditional testing environments and thorough coursework.

description: Introduces analytical methods for turning business data into insight. Covers data preparation, exploratory analysis, predictive modeling, and communicating results. Students work with real datasets using modern tools to support decision making in marketing, operations, and finance.

professor.name: Adam Molnar
professor.aiSummary: Professor Adam Molnar teaches in the Business Department and is known for delivering courses with high difficulty and a heavy workload. While his grading is considered balanced, the curriculum is primarily project-based and requires significant student commitment. Molnar’s classes are often viewed as challenging due to the intensive nature of these assignments.

description: Develops the theory and practice of financial decision making. Covers time value of money, valuation of stocks and bonds, capital budgeting, capital structure, cost of capital, and the relationship between risk and return. Foundational course for the finance major.

professor.name: Scott Cederburg
professor.aiSummary: Professor Scott Cederburg leads a challenging academic environment characterized by a high workload and rigorous grading standards. Cederburg primarily evaluates student performance through exams, requiring a strong command of the course material to succeed. Students should expect a demanding curriculum that prioritizes formal assessments over smaller assignments.

description: Introduces students to concepts of computer technology and the impact of the Internet on social, organizational, personal, and ethical issues. Provides hands-on experience with productivity software (spreadsheets, databases) and develops a critical understanding of information systems.

professor.name: Mark Patton
professor.topReview: Dr. Patton's class was boring and i didn't learn anything AT ALL. The ABPs are busy work, but u'll get full points even if half of what you wrote doesnt make sense,which is what i did. Exams are heavy because you have to sit down and memorize about 7 previous exams. Use quizlet for icqs and pars. Do icqs at home cause u wont learn anything in class
professor.aiSummary: Professor Mark Patton teaches Art History and maintains an overall rating of 4.2. His courses feature a medium workload with project-based assessments and lenient grading standards. While students find the lectures less engaging, Patton offers a low-difficulty experience where consistent effort on projects and exam memorization typically leads to high marks.

description: Survey of contemporary marketing concepts and practices. Examines the role of marketing in the firm and society, the marketing mix (product, price, place, promotion), consumer behavior, market segmentation, and the development of marketing strategy.

professor.name: Christopher Moran
professor.aiSummary: Christopher Moran is new to teaching this course at UA — no student reviews are available yet, so the professor profile is shown with a neutral baseline.

description: Examines the psychological, sociological, and cultural factors that influence consumer decision making. Topics include perception, learning, motivation, attitudes, group influence, and the consumer decision process. Applies consumer behavior theory to marketing strategy.

professor.name: Victor Piscitello
professor.topReview: This class is purely an outlet for Vic to gloat about himself, a huge ego stroke. His primary intention when grading is to find the most petty issues and mark of large portions of grade. He will call you out in the middle of class, just to humiliate you for not paying attention or even not knowing the answer to his question. He'll screw you over...
professor.aiSummary: Professor Victor Piscitello leads an exam-based course with a moderate workload and balanced grading standards. Students describe Piscitello as having a demanding classroom presence, noting that he may call on individuals directly during lectures. Feedback suggests he maintains a strict focus on detail when evaluating assessments, requiring students to be thorough.

description: Principles and practice of professional selling and sales management. Covers the sales process, prospecting, presentations, handling objections, closing, account management, and the recruitment, training, and motivation of sales personnel.

professor.name: Xinying Hao
professor.topReview: This class was kind of a letdown because I did not learn much. This professor requires you to write down "contribution notes" for each class for your participation grade, which makes you focus on that instead of the actual lecture. The guidelines for this course were very confusing and this professor seemed confused as well, however, she is nice!
professor.aiSummary: Professor Xinying Hao leads a project-based course characterized by a heavy workload and strict grading standards. Hao utilizes specific participation requirements, such as daily contribution notes, which some students find distracting from the primary lecture material. While described as a kind instructor, her course guidelines can be difficult for students to navigate.

courseTitle: Optimization and Decision Support Modeling for Business
description: Introduction to operations and supply chain management. Covers process analysis, capacity planning, inventory management, quality management, lean operations, and supply chain design. Emphasizes how operations decisions create value for customers and the firm.

professor.name: Paul Kealey
professor.aiSummary: Professor Paul Kealey teaches in the Department of Business and maintains a 4.3/5 rating. He utilizes a project-based assessment style with a medium workload and balanced grading standards. Students generally find that Kealey manages a moderate level of course difficulty while providing a practical, hands-on learning environment.

description: Develops written and oral business communication skills. Students practice writing professional emails, memos, reports, and proposals, and deliver effective business presentations. Emphasizes audience analysis, clarity, persuasion, and ethical communication.

professor.name: Kevin Cassell
professor.topReview: TOOK BCOM 314, Kevin is one of the better BCOM professors. He's definitely a character but you'll like him. BCOM is just a tougher graded class in general bc you have to Exceed Expectations to get an A, doing what you usually think is enough will only reach a C but gives clear instructions on how to do that. Very helpful, entertaining, nice.
professor.aiSummary: Professor Kevin Cassell is a mathematics instructor known for his engaging and entertaining teaching style. He maintains a project-based curriculum with a manageable workload, though students should strictly follow his detailed instructions to achieve high grades. While Cassell is considered a lenient grader, he sets clear expectations for those aiming to excel in his courses.

description: Foundational course on the theory and practice of management. Covers planning, organizing, leading, and controlling; organizational behavior; leadership; teams; motivation; and the management of change. Prepares students to work effectively in modern organizations.

professor.name: Mark Stegeman
professor.topReview: Before I took this class I was worried when I saw his reviews, but figured I could work hard and get an A. Believe me when I saw that is close to impossible. This professors exams are worded so weirdly, and on top of that he asks the most random questions. He asks more questions about what he rambled about in class rather than what's on the slides.
professor.aiSummary: Mark Stegeman is a business professor whose courses emphasize project-based assessments and a medium workload. Although course material is generally considered accessible, Stegeman is known for highly difficult exams that feature unconventional wording and niche lecture topics. Students often find his grading expectations challenging to meet despite the balanced grading style.

description: Examines management practice across cultural, economic, and political contexts. Topics include cultural intelligence, ethics, sustainability, global strategy, and the social responsibilities of business. Develops the perspective and skills needed to manage in a global environment.

professor.name: Paul Melendez
professor.topReview: Great professor and lecturer. You are graded on four memos and three exams. If follow the template you'll receive an A on the memos. The tests are not cumulative, they follow a certain set of modules, and they are open note. But there are things like case-studies that are not in the notes, so attendance is encouraged to have all the class material.
professor.aiSummary: Professor Paul Melendez is a business instructor recognized for providing an engaging lecture experience. Students are assessed through four templates-based memos and three non-cumulative, open-note exams that focus on specific course modules. While the workload is manageable, Melendez incorporates case studies not found in written notes, making consistent attendance essential for success.

description: Strategic management and policy. Integrates knowledge from across the business disciplines to analyze how firms create and sustain competitive advantage. Uses the case method to examine industry analysis, business and corporate strategy, and strategy implementation.

professor.name: Yong Ge
professor.aiSummary: Professor Yong Ge teaches in the Department of Business and maintains a medium level of course difficulty. While Ge utilizes a project-based assessment style, students should prepare for a high workload with a balanced grading approach. This combination requires consistent effort and organization to manage the substantial tasks involved throughout the semester.

description: Survey of the legal environment of business. Covers contracts, torts, agency, business organizations, employment law, intellectual property, and government regulation. Develops the ability to recognize legal issues in business decisions and to work effectively with legal counsel.

professor.name: Laura Brandimarte
professor.aiSummary: Professor Laura Brandimarte is a highly-rated instructor within the business department who maintains a balanced medium workload and difficulty level. Brandimarte utilizes a project-based assessment style and is noted for being a lenient grader. Students generally find her courses engaging and well-structured, as reflected in her strong overall rating.
